data.title: Texas ventures acquisition III's Class A ordinary shares and warrants to trade separately
data.description:
• Texas Ventures Acquisition III (NASDAQ:TVACU) said on Wednesday that its Class A ordinary shares and warrants  will trade separately from May 16, 2025.
  • The Class A ordinary shares and warrants that are separated will trade on the Nasdaq Global Market under the symbols “TVA” and “TVACW,” respectively.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Vaxart stock climbs on positive study of pill-based norovirus vaccine
data.description:
Vaxart (NASDAQ:VXRT) shares rose in post market trading on Wednesday after the company said it met its main goals in mid-stage trial of its oral pill norovirus vaccine candidate.

The pill-based vaccine met five out of six primary goals, consistent with earlier interim data.

The vaccine was immunogenic and protected against norovirus infection. It reduced norovirus infections by 30% compared to a placebo. While there was also a 21% drop in cases of norovirus-related stomach illness, the difference was not statistically significant.

Using machine learning in the study, researchers identified key immune markers, such as fecal IgA and serum blocking antibodies, as reliable indicators of protection. 

The vaccine was safe and well-tolerated after norovirus challenge, the company said. No serious side effects or dose-limiting issues were reported, and most adverse events were mild.

The stock is up 6% after the bell.

data.title: Boot Barn rallies on FQ1 forecast, same store sales
data.description:
Boot Barn (NYSE:BOOT) shares are rallying in after-hours trading after the company’s upbeat outlook for FQ1, solid comparable store sales, and stock repurchase plan eclipsed a miss on both the company’s fiscal Q4 top- and bottom-line and lackluster outlook for FY26.

“Our team delivered a solid finish to fiscal year 2025 highlighted by 15% annual total sales growth and 23% growth in earnings per diluted share, underscoring the ongoing resilience of our core consumer despite broader market uncertainties,” Boot Barn CEO John Hazen said in a statement, adding that the company remains confident in its ability to navigate the current tariff environment thanks to a diversified supply chain and well-established vendor relationships.

“The fundamentals of our business remain strong, and we are well-positioned to continue generating value for our shareholders,” Hazen added.

Led by a 9.8% gain in e-commerce same store sales, company-wide comparable sales were up 6.0%, which was below expectations but significantly better than negative 8.7% in the same quarter last year. The increased traffic led to a 16.8% increase in sales YOY to $453.7M, $4.65M below expectation. The company’s profitability also improved by 27% to $1.22 per share, although 2 cents shy of estimates.  Gross profit margin improved 120 basis points to 37.1%, largely due to an increase in sales and merchandise margin.

Looking ahead to FY26, Boot Barn (NYSE:BOOT) expects to earn a profit between $5.50 to $6.40 per share, of which the midpoint of $5.95 is below the $6.39 estimate. Net sales are pegged for $2.07B to $2.15B, representing growth of 8% to 13%, also less than the $2.17B estimate.

Other forecasts for FY26 include same store sales to be down 2% to up 2%, a merchandise margin of 49.8% to 50.1%, and gross profit margin between 36.1% and 36.9%.

For fiscal Q1, the company expects a profit of $1.44 to $1.52 per share, with a midpoint of $1.48 that is 2 cents better than estimates. Total sales are estimated to be between $483M and $491M, representing growth of 14% to 16% and better than the estimated $486.1M. Same store sales growth is expected to be up 4% to 6%, merchandise margin of 51.7% and gross profit margin of 27.9% to 38.2%.

Finally, the company’s Board of Directors has authorized the Company to repurchase up to $200 million of its common stock.

Boot Barn (NYSE:BOOT) shares are up more than 17%.

data.title: Gold slides for fourth time in six sessions as risk appetite dulls appeal of safe-haven assets
data.description:
Gold futures fell to the lowest in more than a month Wednesday as tensions eased in global trade, pushing investors away from safe-haven demand and reducing gold's appeal, at least for now.

Gold sold off sharply at the beginning of the week as the steep reduction in U.S.-China tariffs for 90 days while trade talks continue sparked risk-on sentiment, then broadly stabilized on Tuesday as the latest U.S. data showed inflation rising less than expected in April, raising optimism for interest rate cuts; lower rates typically increase the appeal of non-interest bearing bullion.

"With tariff rollbacks on both sides and negotiations showing real progress, investors are getting more comfortable," Forex.com analyst Fawad Razaqzada wrote. "What's more, Tuesday's softer than expected U.S. inflation print added fuel to the fire, calming nerves that Trump's trade tariffs would trigger a fresh inflation wave," although long-term bullish prospects remain intact.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for May delivery closed -1.8% to $3,181.40/oz, its fourth loss in the past six sessions and lowest settlement value since April 10, and front-month May Comex silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended -1.9% to $32.226/oz, its lowest since May 5.

Gold touched a record high $3,500/oz last month during the height of trade fears, and the metal is still up 21% YTD and 33% in the past year.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: IPM signals growth in cloud infrastructure and cybersecurity through NTS acquisition and new referral pipeline
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Intelligent Protection Management Corp. (IPM) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Jason Katz, Chief Executive Officer, described the first quarter of 2025 as "transformational for IPM following the closing of our acquisition of NTS, which focused our business on the rapidly growing cloud infrastructure and cybersecurity sectors." He highlighted the company's shift to a standalone integrated service model, emphasizing opportunities to expand service offerings to legacy NTS customers and cross-sell ManyCam software and new services to the existing web hosting base.
  • Katz stated, "We expect that the recently announced referral arrangement with NewtekOne, a current client, and a financial holding company with tens of thousands of its own business clients, has great potential to help us find new customers."
  • Katz noted a "strong balance sheet highlighted by $9.7 million in cash and cash equivalents and no long-term debt."
  • Katz announced "effective May 8, 2025, our Board of Directors approved a stock repurchase plan for up to $400,000 of the company’s outstanding common stock."
  • Katz provided an update on ongoing patent litigation, reporting a jury award of $65.7 million in their favor against Cisco, though he cautioned, "we estimate that we would receive no more than one-third of the gross proceeds in connection with the award subject to post-trial proceedings, including any potential appellate proceedings by Cisco. We have not recorded any gained contingency in connection with the award."
  • Kara Jenny, Chief Financial Officer, reported, "Total revenue for the three months ended March 31, 2025 was $5.5 million, compared to $0.3 million for the three months ended March 31, 2024. This increase was primarily driven by new revenue streams as a result of the acquisition of NTS."

Outlook

  • Katz stated, "We believe we are now well positioned to grow the company through the expansion of our services offerings to existing legacy NTS customers, while cross-selling our ManyCam software and varying new services to our historical web hosting customer base. This should lead to further growth and enhancement of operational efficiencies, all focused on driving stockholder value."
  • Katz identified "potential for merger and acquisition opportunities to further scale our growth" and indicated plans to incorporate AI features into Secure Private Cloud offerings in the future.
  • The referral arrangement with NewtekOne is expected to "help us find new customers."

Financial Results

  • Jenny reported, "Loss from continuing operations for the three months ended March 31, 2025 was $1.3 million, compared to a loss from continuing operations of $1.0 million for the three months ended March 31, 2024."
  • The company incurred approximately $0.3 million of one-time expenses related to the transaction in Q1 2025, and the loss from continuing operations included $0.9 million of non-cash expense (amortization and depreciation of $0.7 million, $0.4 million of which was related to newly acquired intangible assets, and $0.2 million of non-cash share-based compensation).
  • Jenny reported, "Net income for the three months ended March 31, 2025 was $0.8 million, compared to a net loss of $0.5 million for the three months ended March 31, 2024. Net income included a tax benefit of $2.1 million in connection with the recognition of tax benefits recorded in connection with the transactions for the three months ended March 31, 2025."
  • Adjusted EBITDA remained at negative $0.5 million for both Q1 2025 and the prior year period.
  • Net cash generated from operating activities for the quarter was $1.7 million.
  • Cash and cash equivalents totaled $9.7 million at March 31, 2025, a decrease of $0.9 million from December 31, 2024, due to a $4 million cash payment as part of the NTS acquisition, partially offset by $1.4 million of cash inflow from the divestiture of transferred assets and $1.7 million of cash flow from operations.

Q&A

  • Kara Jenny asked Katz, "Since you have now owned this business for four plus months, can you discuss what you have done or put in place to grow and expand this business? Have you made additional sales and marketing hires?" Katz responded, "We hired a company that focuses on marketing, search engine optimization, pay-per-click advertising, branding and email marketing management. In addition, we have allocated marketing spend to enable a CRM system that allows us to email our customer database of approximately 40,000 current and former customers and track their open rates and response to our email."
  • Jenny asked, "Can you tell us roughly how many of your clients currently utilize just one of your Managed Technology offerings? And as a follow-on to that, have you begun to cross-sell additional offerings to your clients?" Katz replied, "Yeah. We have started to email and cross-sell our Managed Technology offerings to the ManyCam user base, as well as offering our Web Hosting customers all of our additional solutions."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was neutral, focusing on practical questions about sales and marketing hires and cross-selling progress.
  • Management’s tone was confident during prepared remarks, with Katz stating, "We are very excited with the prospect of expanding our Managed Technology Solutions business."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s confidence shifted from anticipation about the transition to concrete actions post-NTS acquisition and integration, while analysts remained neutral.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The acquisition of NTS was completed in January and drove a substantial increase in revenue from $0.3 million in Q4 2024 to $5.5 million in Q1 2025.
  • The company shifted from discussing anticipated growth and integration to reporting on actual integration steps and early cross-selling efforts.
  • Management’s tone remained optimistic, but the focus moved from transaction execution to operational execution, cross-selling, and marketing investment for growth.
  • The previous quarter highlighted challenges with professional fees and transaction costs, while the current quarter emphasized new revenue streams, marketing initiatives, and an approved stock repurchase plan.

Risks and Concerns

  • Katz reiterated that the final proceeds from the Cisco litigation may be reduced by legal fees and post-trial proceedings, with no contingency gain recorded yet.
  • The company reported one-time transaction expenses and non-cash expenses in connection with the NTS acquisition and integration.
  • Management indicated a continued focus on expanding offerings and integrating new operations to drive efficiencies and growth.

Final Takeaway

IPM’s management underscored the first quarter of 2025 as a pivotal period, marked by the NTS acquisition and a clear pivot toward cloud infrastructure and cybersecurity. With a robust balance sheet, ongoing integration efforts, a new customer referral pipeline, and an active marketing strategy, the company communicated confidence in its ability to drive revenue growth and operational efficiencies while remaining mindful of transaction-related risks and the uncertain timing of potential litigation proceeds.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
